title: Energizing Flaky Algerian Cheese Calzone
description: A flexitarian-friendly, energizing Algerian dish featuring the floral flavors of sage and a delicious flambe cheese pull.
instructions: Roll out the flaky pastry dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and cook the garlic until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chopped sage to the skillet and cook for an additional 30 seconds, until wilted.
In a separate bowl, combine the grated Kasseri cheese and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Place the cooked sage and garlic mixture on one half of the pastry dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Spread the cheese mixture over the filling, making sure to stay within the borders.
Brush the edges of the pastry with a little water and fold the other half of the dough over the filling, pressing the edges to seal.
Use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
Place the calzone on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the top with a little olive oil.
Cut a few slits in the top of the calzone to allow steam to escape during baking.
Bake the calzone in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown and puffed.
Allow the calzone to cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
If desired, sprinkle with additional chopped sage before serving.
